patches.summary: Adds Beehiiv-specific read-only insight commands and warning handling for partial failures.
patches.reason: The generated OpenAPI surface exposes raw endpoints, but Beehiiv users need compact growth, source, post, field, referral, and subscriber lookup workflows.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and verify-skill pass for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Restricts Beehiiv response-cache directories and files to the current user.
patches.reason: Beehiiv API responses can contain subscriber emails and other PII, so cache files must not be world-readable on multi-user systems.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Bypasses response and offline caches for short-lived subscriber JWT token reads.
patches.reason: Beehiiv subscriber auto-login JWTs are short lived and stale cached tokens can produce unusable login URLs.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Escapes generated CLI, MCP, and compound insight path parameters before inserting them into URL paths.
patches.reason: Beehiiv email and publication path parameters can contain reserved characters, and raw replacement breaks valid API paths.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Removes dead generated scaffolding from refresh-token and update-by-email paths.
patches.reason: Beehiiv uses bearer-token auth in this CLI, so unsupported OAuth refresh tokens should fail explicitly instead of silently no-oping; empty generated conditionals should not ship.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Routes workflow archive through the generated sync endpoint map.
patches.reason: Beehiiv user and workspace identity resources live at /users/identify and /workspaces/identify, not collection paths.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.

patches.summary: Resolves multi-placeholder dependent sync paths using intermediate resource IDs and their publication parent IDs.
patches.reason: Nested Beehiiv resources such as automation emails, segment members, poll responses, and newsletter-list subscriptions require both the intermediate resource ID and publication ID; leaving either placeholder unresolved causes invalid API paths.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes for the published Beehiiv CLI.
